description:
On VBAT application, the BMS shall:
  · Initialise all subsystems
  · Perform memory self-check
  · Load calibration parameters from NVM
  · Verify sensor communication
Upon successful initialisation: BMS_SystemState := Standby
supplementary_info:
Function: BMS State Machine
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection > 12 Fault Management > 12.1 Unrecoverable Fault Handling > 13 BMS State Machine > 13.1 Power-Up and Initialization
Context: The BMS state machine defines the operating modes of the battery system and governs transitions between them. All protection functions (sections 1-12) reference the current system state for context-appropriate response. Initialisation on power-up performs self-checks before entering Standby:
- BMS_SystemState: 0 = Off, 1 = Standby, 2 = PrechargeActive, 3 = HV_Active, 4 = HV_Deactivating, 5 = SafeState, 6 = HV_Failure
- Wake-up sources: CAN activity or dedicated wake-up pin
- Initialisation target: 500 ms from wake to CAN report

analysis.direction: none found
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the BMS initialization sequence including subsystems, memory self-check, calibration parameter loading, and sensor communication verification.
Verify that after successful initialization, BMS_SystemState transitions to Standby (1).
Ensure that the BMS reports its state within 500 ms of wake-up.

cases.title: Initialization sequence upon VBAT application including subsystems, memory self-check, calibration parameter loading, and sensor communication verification
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS initializes all subsystems, performs a memory self-check, loads calibration parameters from NVM, and verifies sensor communication as part of its initialization sequence.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults.
cases.postcondition: BMS returned to normal operating state with BMS_SystemState set to 1 (Standby).

steps.action: Apply VBAT to the BMS.
steps.expected: The BMS shall initialize all subsystems and set BMS_SystemState to 0 (Off).

steps.action: Wait for 500 ms.
steps.expected: [NEEDS REVIEW] The BMS shall perform a memory self-check, load calibration parameters from NVM, and verify sensor communication. BMS_SystemState remains at 0 (Off).

steps.action: Wait for r_CellOV_Threshold ms after the initial self-check.
steps.expected: [NEEDS REVIEW] The BMS shall transition to BMS_SystemState 1 (Standby) upon successful completion of initialization and self-checks.

cases.raw_html:
<testcase>
<title>Initialization sequence upon VBAT application including subsystems, memory self-check, calibration parameter loading, and sensor communication verification</title>
<objective>To verify that the BMS initializes all subsystems, performs a memory self-check, loads calibration parameters from NVM, and verifies sensor communication as part of its initialization sequence.</objective>
<related_requirement>REQ-BMS-STM-001</related_requirement>
<precondition>BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults.</precondition>
<steps>
<step order="1">
<action>Apply VBAT to the BMS.</action>
<expected>The BMS shall initialize all subsystems and set BMS_SystemState to 0 (Off).</expected>
</step>
<step order="2">
<action>Wait for 500 ms.</action>
<expected>[NEEDS REVIEW] The BMS shall perform a memory self-check, load calibration parameters from NVM, and verify sensor communication. BMS_SystemState remains at 0 (Off).</expected>
</step>
<step order="3">
<action>Wait for r_CellOV_Threshold ms after the initial self-check.</action>
<expected>[NEEDS REVIEW] The BMS shall transition to BMS_SystemState 1 (Standby) upon successful completion of initialization and self-checks.</expected>
</step>
</steps>
<postcondition>BMS returned to normal operating state with BMS_SystemState set to 1 (Standby).</postcondition>
</testcase>

description:
IF  unrecoverable internal fault detected
THEN
  · Transition to SafeState
  · Open all contactors
  · BMS_Fault_Latch := 1
  · Broadcast fault status on CAN
supplementary_info:
Function: Fault Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection > 12 Fault Management > 12.1 Unrecoverable Fault Handling
Context: Unrecoverable internal faults represent failures from which the BMS cannot safely continue normal operation. These include hardware integrity failures and critical sensor path loss. Recovery from this state requires a power cycle, and the fault code is retained for diagnostic readout:
- Memory error (ECC uncorrectable), critical sensor failure, watchdog timeout
- Response: SafeState, all contactors open, BMS_Fault_Latch = 1, power cycle required

description:
Upon VCU_HVConnect_Req = 1 with all preconditions satisfied:
  · Close negative main contactor
  · Enable precharge circuit
  · BMS_SystemState := PrechargeActive
supplementary_info:
Function: BMS State Machine
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection > 12 Fault Management > 12.1 Unrecoverable Fault Handling > 13 BMS State Machine > 13.1 Power-Up and Initialization > 13.2 HV Activation
Context: HV activation follows a precharge sequence to safely energise the DC link before closing the positive contactor, preventing inrush current damage. The sequence is aborted if the bus voltage does not rise within the expected time. Up to 2 retries are permitted before locking out HV activation until power cycle:
- Preconditions: VCU_HVConnect_Req = 1, no active faults, isolation OK, contactors Open, pack voltage in range
- Success criteria: DC bus >= 90 % of pack voltage within precharge timeout
- Failure: BMS_SystemState = HV_Failure, BMS_PrechargeFault_Flag = 1

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_SystemState := PrechargeActive; clearing: BMS_SystemState != PrechargeActive, BMS_PrechargeFault_Flag = 1
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where VCU_HVConnect_Req is asserted and all preconditions are satisfied, leading to successful precharge activation.

Test the boundary condition for the precharge timeout duration. Verify that if the DC bus voltage does not rise within 90% of the pack voltage by t_precharge_timeout, BMS_SystemState should transition to HV_Failure and BMS_PrechargeFault_Flag should be set.

Test the fault condition where VCU_HVConnect_Req is asserted but preconditions are not met (e.g., active faults present). Ensure that BMS_SystemState does not transition to PrechargeActive and BMS_PrechargeFault_Flag remains 0.

Test the state transitions when VCU_HVConnect_Req is de-asserted or preconditions are no longer satisfied during the precharge sequence. Ensure that BMS_SystemState returns to a safe state (e.g., NormalOperation) and BMS_PrechargeFault_Flag is appropriately set.

Verify the observability of the system by checking if the CAN bus correctly reports the status of VCU_HVConnect_Req, BMS_SystemState, and BMS_PrechargeFault_Flag during precharge activation and failure scenarios.

description:
IF  calculated SOH <= 80 %
THEN
  · BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn := 1
  · Store DTC P0A0F in non-volatile memory
supplementary_info:
Function: SOC/SOH Estimation
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring
Context: SOH degradation tracking enables early detection of battery aging and supports warranty analysis. Degradation is evaluated through multiple indicators including capacity fade and cumulative throughput. When the design cycle limit is exceeded, an SOH re-calibration is requested:
- BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn = 1: SOH <= 80 %
- BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag = 1: cumulative throughput exceeds limit, DTC P0A0F stored

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn := 1 when SOH <= 80 %
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where calculated SOH is exactly 80%, triggering BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn.
Test the boundary condition where calculated SOH is slightly less than 80%, ensuring BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn does not trigger.
Test the scenario where cumulative throughput exceeds limit, verifying DTC P0A0F is stored in non-volatile memory.
Verify that after BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn := 1, no further changes to this signal are observed under normal conditions (assuming no other triggering events).

description:
During active balancing:
  · Selected cells shall be discharged through balancing resistor
  · Balancing shall continue until delta < 20 mV or timeout reached
  · Balancing shall be suspended when pack current > 5 A
supplementary_info:
Function: Cell Balancing
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation

analysis.direction: triggering: balancing shall continue until delta < 20 mV or timeout reached; clearing: balancing shall be suspended when pack current > 5 A
missing_critical_info: none

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior of cell balancing where the balancing continues until the delta voltage is less than 20 mV or a timeout occurs.
Test the boundary condition for the delta cell voltage threshold (i.e., when the delta voltage is exactly 20 mV).
Test the boundary condition for the pack current threshold (i.e., when the pack current is exactly 5 A).

description:
IF  DC bus voltage >= 90 % of pack voltage within precharge timeout
THEN
  · Close positive main contactor
  · Disable precharge circuit
  · BMS_SystemState := HV_Active
ELSE
  · Abort HV activation
  · BMS_SystemState := HV_Failure
  · BMS_PrechargeFault_Flag := 1

description:
IF  pack charge current exceeds 150 A
THEN
  · BMS_ChargeOC_Flag := 1
  · Limit charge current to 100 A within 50 ms
supplementary_info:
Function: Current Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection
Context: Overcurrent protection monitors charge and discharge currents independently, as they imply different hazard profiles. Charge overcurrent primarily risks cell overvoltage (see section 1), while discharge overcurrent may indicate a load-side fault. All thresholds are calibration parameters:
- BMS_ChargeOC_Flag = 1: charge current > 150 A
- BMS_DischargeOC_Flag = 1: discharge current > 200 A
- Recovery ramp: power restored at max 10 % per second after 2 s hold

missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

Test that the system limits the charge current to 100 A within 50 ms when BMS_ChargeOC_Flag is set to 1.
Test the recovery process where BMS_ChargeOC_Flag transitions from 1 back to 0 after the pack charge current drops below 150 A and remains low for a sufficient duration.

description:
Upon VCU_HVDisconnect_Req or critical fault:
  · Open both main contactors
  · Discharge DC link capacitance
  · BMS_SystemState := HV_Deactivating
  · THEN Standby or SafeState as appropriate
supplementary_info:
Function: BMS State Machine
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection > 12 Fault Management > 12.1 Unrecoverable Fault Handling > 13 BMS State Machine > 13.1 Power-Up and Initialization > 13.2 HV Activation > 13.3 HV Deactivation and Sleep
Context: HV deactivation is triggered by normal shutdown requests or fault conditions from any protection function (sections 1-12). After contactors open and the DC link discharges, the system may enter low-power sleep mode to conserve quiescent current while maintaining wake-up capability:
- Deactivation: open contactors, discharge DC link, transition through HV_Deactivating to Standby or SafeState
- Sleep entry: Standby with no CAN activity for configurable timeout
- Sleep exit: CAN activity or wake pin assertion

analysis.direction: The requirement describes a triggering behavior: upon VCU_HVDisconnect_Req or critical fault, the system transitions through specific states.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

Verify that the system correctly transitions through HV_Deactivating state before entering Standby or SafeState.
Ensure proper handling of a critical fault during deactivation, including contactor opening and DC link discharge.
Confirm that the BMS_SystemState reflects the correct state after HV deactivation (HV_Deactivating, Standby, SafeState).

description:
IF  CAN bus-off or excessive error passive detected
THEN
  · BMS_CANFault_Flag := 1
  · Enter limp-home mode (discharge power <= 30 %)
  · BMS_SafeState_Entry := 1
supplementary_info:
Function: Communication & Diagnostics
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault
Context: CAN communication faults compromise the BMS ability to coordinate with VCU and other vehicle systems. Upon fault detection, the BMS enters a limp-home mode with reduced functionality while maintaining essential safety monitoring. Normal operation resumes when communication is restored and the fault condition clears:
- BMS_CANFault_Flag = 1: CAN bus-off or excessive error passive detected
- BMS_SafeState_Entry = 1: request safe state entry
- Limp-home mode: discharge limited to 30 %, basic safety functions maintained

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CANFault_Flag is set to 1 when CAN bus-off or excessive error passive is detected. BMS_SafeState_Entry is triggered, indicating a request for safe state entry.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

description:
IF  total pack voltage < minimum operating threshold
THEN
  · BMS_PackUV_Flag := 1
  · Send DTC P0A01 to VCU
  · Limit discharge power proportionally to undervoltage severity
supplementary_info:
Function: Pack Voltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring
Context: Pack voltage monitoring provides a system-level check that complements individual cell monitoring (section 8). A pack undervoltage or sensor plausibility fault is reported to the VCU with DTC. The pack voltage measurement is cross-checked against the sum of cell voltages to detect sensor faults:
- BMS_PackUV_Flag = 1: pack voltage < minimum operating threshold, DTC P0A01 sent
- BMS_PackVoltSensorFault_Flag = 1: cell-sum to pack measurement deviation > 5 V
